rationale: Neither candidate surfaces the one substantive human concern (yenuo26's question about verifying the reduce_scatter parameter more thoroughly in the mock test), so recall is near-zero for both. X's five comments are individually grounded (real line refs, htmlcov coverage data) but are low-value test-coverage/style nits, are internally redundant (points 1 and 5 both rehash the same guard-branch observation), and the whole block is bizarrely triplicated verbatim — then capped with an unwarranted REQUEST CHANGES verdict for what the actual reviewers approved and merged. Y is concise, verifies the real technical premises (DPMetadata construction, init_vllm_model_parallel_group semantics, group cleanup/destroy paths) against the upstream vLLM source, correctly lands on APPROVE matching the actual outcome, and offers one well-reasoned non-blocking comment about the dp_metadata caching/collective invariant. Y is better calibrated and equally actionable despite far less bulk.
